goals.short_name: British Columbians in need have access to income and disability assistance

main_services.name: Apply for and manage income assistance
Apply for and manage disability assistance
Get financial support as a senior or through the bus pass program

goals.short_name: People with disabilities get community living supports and a more accessible B.C.

main_services.name: Get community living and disability supports
Advance accessibility and service quality across government

goals.short_name: British Columbians facing employment barriers find and keep good jobs

main_services.name: Get help finding and keeping a job

goals.short_name: Poverty is reduced and British Columbians are more socially included

main_services.name: Track and shape B.C.'s Poverty Reduction Strategy

acts.summary: Assented to May 30, 2002. Governs income assistance and hardship assistance for people without the Persons with Disabilities designation, employment-related programs, reconsideration and appeal rights, and related supplements such as the Senior's Supplement and the BC Bus Pass Program. Confirmed current to November 5, 2024 on BC Laws.

sections.title: Income assistance and supplements
sections.description: Sets out eligibility for income assistance, hardship assistance, and supplements provided under the Act.

services.name: Apply for income assistance
services.ms_name: Apply for and manage income assistance

services.name: Get emergency hardship assistance
services.ms_name: Apply for and manage income assistance

sections.title: Reconsideration and appeal rights
sections.description: Allows applicants and recipients to request reconsideration of a decision and, if dissatisfied, appeal to the Employment and Assistance Appeal Tribunal.

services.name: Ask for reconsideration of an assistance decision
services.ms_name: Apply for and manage income assistance

sections.title: Senior's Supplement
sections.description: Provides the regulatory basis for the automatic monthly Senior's Supplement top-up for low-income seniors receiving OAS/GIS.

services.name: Get a monthly top-up to your federal seniors' pension
services.ms_name: Get financial support as a senior or through the bus pass program

sections.title: BC Bus Pass Program
sections.description: Provides the regulatory basis for subsidized annual bus passes for low-income seniors and people on disability assistance.

services.name: Get a reduced-cost bus pass as a senior or person with a disability
services.ms_name: Get financial support as a senior or through the bus pass program

sections.title: Employment-related programs
sections.description: Authorizes the minister to establish or fund employment-related programs, including WorkBC Employment Services Centres and the Single Parent Employment Initiative.

services.name: Get help finding a job at an employment services centre
services.ms_name: Get help finding and keeping a job

services.name: Get training and support as a single parent to secure long-term employment
services.ms_name: Get help finding and keeping a job

acts.full_name: Employment and Assistance for Persons with Disabilities Act
acts.summary: Assented to May 30, 2002. Governs the Persons with Disabilities (PWD) designation and disability assistance, hardship assistance and supplements for people with disabilities. Confirmed current to December 31, 2024 on BC Laws.

sections.title: Persons with disabilities designation
sections.description: Sets the criteria the minister uses to designate a person as a Person with Disabilities, including the simplified/prescribed-class application route.

services.name: Apply for the Persons with Disabilities designation
services.ms_name: Apply for and manage disability assistance

services.name: Use a simplified application if you already receive disability-related benefits
services.ms_name: Apply for and manage disability assistance

sections.title: Disability assistance and supplements
sections.description: Authorizes the minister to provide disability assistance or a supplement to an eligible family unit that includes a person with disabilities.

services.name: Apply for disability assistance
services.ms_name: Apply for and manage disability assistance

acts.summary: Establishes a framework for government to work with people with disabilities and the community to identify, remove, and prevent accessibility barriers, including accessibility standards, a government accessibility plan, and a public feedback mechanism. Citation corroborated via CanLII listing and the Accessibility Directorate's page on gov.bc.ca; direct BC Laws document rendering was not accessible during this research pass.

sections.title: Accessibility feedback mechanisms
sections.description: Requires a process for receiving and responding to public feedback on accessibility barriers.

services.name: Give feedback on accessibility barriers in government services
services.ms_name: Advance accessibility and service quality across government

sections.title: Accessibility standards and government accessibility plan
sections.description: Provides for development of accessibility standards and regulations, and requires a government accessibility plan.

services.name: Track government's progress on accessibility standards and planning
services.ms_name: Advance accessibility and service quality across government

acts.summary: Requires government to maintain a poverty reduction strategy with legislated targets, timelines and an annual progress report. Amended in 2024 to set new 2034 targets (60% overall, 75% child, 50% seniors' poverty reduction, using 2016 as baseline). Citation corroborated via CanLII listing; direct BC Laws document rendering was not accessible during this research pass.

sections.title: Targets, timelines and accountability
sections.description: Sets the legislated poverty-reduction targets and timelines, and requires annual progress reports and an independent advisory committee.

services.name: Track progress on BC's Poverty Reduction Strategy targets
services.ms_name: Track and shape B.C.'s Poverty Reduction Strategy

services.name: Take part in an advisory committee on poverty reduction
services.ms_name: Track and shape B.C.'s Poverty Reduction Strategy

acts.summary: Establishes Community Living BC (CLBC) as a provincial Crown agency responsible for planning and funding supports for adults with developmental disabilities, autism spectrum disorder, and fetal alcohol spectrum disorder. Citation corroborated via CanLII and Wikipedia listings; direct BC Laws document rendering was not accessible during this research pass.

sections.title: Establishment of Community Living BC
sections.description: Establishes CLBC and its mandate to plan, fund, and monitor supports for eligible adults.

services.name: Get community living supports as an adult with a developmental disability
services.ms_name: Get community living and disability supports

services.name: Request a review of a community living funding decision or file a complaint
services.ms_name: Get community living and disability supports
